explanation_box.text: He's smiling, which is a trait that potential mates find attractive.
He has one eye that looks deformed, which makes him less attractive to potential mates.
Red species are irregular and less attractive to potential mates.
His disposition looks more welcoming and receptive, which increases the chance of mating.
His feet are strong and large, which is attractive to potential mates.
Her toes are soft and feminine.
The red breed fewer than the green breed.
Red species with round toes produce fewer offspring
This takes places in the fall

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?
